review_findings.t: missed call site: Phase D re-check (workflow.md:488) re-runs reviewers, uncited
internal contradiction on capture timing

divergence: HIGH-RISK: the dev applied the retro's POST-merge two-dot prescription to conduct's PRE-merge review context — backwards. Adversarial review caught it; corrected to merge-base form (Scenario A pre-merge) with the post-merge two-dot documented as the distinct Scenario B. The per-story review diff-range is foundational to the engine's review correctness; shipping the contract-literal pattern would have mis-scoped/emptied every per-story review diff.
contract_leftover: The FROZEN contract itself encodes the wrong model — it literally requires 'capture pre-merge sha at merge point + two-dot', which is the post-merge pattern. The engine-correct implementation deliberately diverges from the contract's literal wording. Frozen contract NOT edited; flagged as a contract-quality leftover to correct in follow-up.

change: Phase-5 request-changes branch: parse -> bounded autonomous fixer pass -> re-render -> return to the SAME end-gate
dev_summary: Implemented the Phase-5 REQUEST-CHANGES loop (5.RC.1-5.RC.6): parse the change request into discrete fixer items, run a bounded autonomous directed-fixer pass (no mid-loop prompt), re-render the end-gate report in place, and re-present the SAME gate (not a new mandatory gate); residuals -> triage stubs.

ive_note: Closes the redispatch loop (the single human gate is re-presented after autonomous fixes, not a new gate). Adversary caught a real budget-halving double-increment and a git-context hazard (the avfl-merge-class wiring risk). The stakes-bypass concern was resolved by classifying requested changes from text + an escalated CASE, so a destructive/security requested change still surfaces as a decision card while ordinary authorized changes stay autonomous.
